summary: Largest Leymebamba khipu read in this session (572 cords, 3 STRING / 315 INT / 254 EMPTY = 44 percent segmentation) at the Centro Mallqui (Laguna de los Condores, catalog INC/LDC 108 LC1947.TEQ) -- a FOURTH sub-collection distinct from CMA-628 and CMA 480. Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED (depth L3). 48 clusters of highly varied size: 25 SINGLETON clusters (phase separators) + 23 DATA CLUSTERS (3-34 cords each). Colors: AB 156, W 126, MB 123, KB 63, GG 32 -- balanced 5-color distribution. ENORMOUS MAGNITUDE: total value 92,449 (including L1+L2+L3 cords; L1 alone = 76,168). Top cluster sums: C6=19816, C43=18249, C17=9243, C4=8981, C8=7225 -- multiple clusters exceeding 7,000 units. This is 30x the total of UR010 (2,980) and 3x the total of UR015 (29,512). 3 STRING markers all carry MASSIVE NUMERICAL PREFIXES: cord 21 (L1 KB) = TOP-cha with S10 prefix; L2 ord 15 (KB) = piqa (summit / who-TOP) with S10 prefix; L2 ord 17 (W) = chi-ACC with a cord-length numerical prefix totaling ~750 across 7 S100 + 5 S10 knots. CLASSIFICATION: numerical_record, promoting the genre from UR087 + UR010 to N=3 with the LARGEST and HIGHEST-MAGNITUDE instance. MASTER REGISTER HYPOTHESIS TESTED AND REJECTED (2026-04-17 session, cross-matching test): UR001 total (92,449) does NOT equal the sum of the other 4 Laguna de los Condores sub-register totals (UR007 + UR010 + UR012 + UR015 = 35,585). Ratio UR001 / sub-sum = 2.60x. No UR001 cluster sum matches any sub-register total within 10 percent (closest is cluster 42 = 3,423 vs UR012 total 3,035 = 13 percent delta). UR001 is therefore INDEPENDENT of the 4 other Laguna de los Condores khipus read so far; it is NOT a simple aggregate of them. Three possibilities remain: (a) UR001 is simply an independent large register; (b) UR001 aggregates OTHER Laguna de los Condores khipus not yet deep-read (27+ of 32 total remain); (c) UR001 covers a different time period or administrative scope. 9 Ascher sums declared but 0/8 verified -- repeats the UR010 anomaly, consistent with possible Chachapoyas non-standard arithmetic. v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE (STRING onsets are L2 / L8 / L9 -- not L10 / L11).
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION (v3, 2026-04-17)
  572 cords. Architecture 1-PAIRE STRUCTURED (depth L3).
  48 clusters of sizes distributed as: 25 singletons (size 1) + 1 size 3 + 1 size 8 + 1 size 11 + 1 size 14 + 4 size 15 + 6 size 16 + 1 size 17 + 1 size 18 + 1 size 20 + 2 size 21 + 2 size 22 + 1 size 34. The bimodal distribution (singletons vs mid-size clusters) indicates a SEPARATOR + DATA-BLOCK design with 23 data clusters.
  Composition: INT 315 (55 percent), STRING 3, EMPTY 254 (44 percent).
  Provenance: Leymebamba, Amazonas, Peru. Museum: Centro Mallqui. Catalog INC/LDC 108, LC1947.TEQ -- DISTINCT from CMA-628 (UR015 + UR012) and CMA 480 (UR007 + UR010). A FOURTH sub-collection within the Laguna de los Condores archive.
  Colors: AB 156 (27 percent), W 126 (22 percent), MB 123 (22 percent), KB 63 (11 percent), GG 32 (6 percent), plus minor. Balanced 5-color distribution -- unusual for a large document.
  KFG twin KH0237.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  3/3 STRING cords decoded (100 percent dict hits): TOP-cha, piqa, chi-ACC (all compound morphologies).
  315 INT values clean, no L?.

ASCHER LAYER
  9 pendant-pendant sums declared in KFG, 0 of 8 verified by sum-constraint check. Cascade depth 3.
  Same ANOMALY pattern as UR010 (68 declared / 0 verified). Both UR001 and UR010 are LARGE numerical registers at Laguna de los Condores with declarative-but-unverified Ascher structures. This cross-document pattern reinforces the possibility of a CHACHAPOYAS-SPECIFIC NON-STANDARD ARITHMETIC SYSTEM that the standard Inca-template Ascher algorithm cannot parse, OR that these large numerical registers were in-progress / unfinished.
  No Pass 3A, no Pass 3D repairs.

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE
  The 48-cluster structure can be decomposed:
    - 25 SINGLETON SEPARATOR CLUSTERS (phase markers, mostly EMPTY).
    - 23 DATA CLUSTERS of 3-34 cords each carrying the numerical register (28 non-zero including L2/L3).
    - 3 STRING cords distributed within the data clusters as CATEGORY LABELS with numerical prefixes.
  ENORMOUS MAGNITUDE: total 92,449 across 315 INT values at all levels (L1 alone = 76,168 across 178 values). Peak values at L1: 8,308 / 8,201 / 5,490 / 4,501 / 4,122 / 4,104 / 4,005 / 3,834 / 3,405 / 3,101. Top cluster sums (including L2/L3): C6=19,816 / C43=18,249 / C17=9,243 / C4=8,981 / C8=7,225 / C40=4,015 / C10=3,689 / C42=3,423 / C28=2,344 / C23=2,189.
  The top 15 individual values all exceed 1,200 and many exceed 4,000 -- magnitudes consistent with AGGREGATE TOTALS rather than per-unit measurements. 92,449 could represent tens of thousands of tribute units, a large population count, or multi-year aggregated production.

VOCABULARY
  TOP-cha (cord 21, L1, KB) -- compound form: knots E S10 L8 = qa (TOP suffix) + 10 (numerical prefix) + cha (L8 onset polyphony per syllabary v3, or na via cha->na rule). Reading: 'topic-cha' or 'the-cha-topic' with numerical value 10 attached.
  piqa (L2 ord 15, KB) -- knots S10 L9 E = 10 (numerical prefix) + pi + qa. Reading: summit / who-TOP with numerical value 10 attached. A subsidiary cord carrying both a count AND a directional marker.
  chi-ACC (L2 ord 17, W) -- knots L2 S100 S10 L5 S100 S10 S100 S10 S100 S10 S100 S10 S100 = chi + [massive numerical prefix: 7x S100 + 5x S10 = approximately 750] + L5 (ta-ACC). Reading: 'chi-...-ta-ACC' = 'the chi [something with numerical content] taken as object'. Dual reading: a text label with a 750-unit numerical prefix attached.
  Total vocabulary: 3 compound words. 100 percent dict hits.
  v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE: STRING onsets are L2 (chi-), L8 (TOP-cha has cha onset = L8), L9 (piqa has pi onset = L9). None are L10 or L11.

VOCAB POSITIONS
  All 3 STRINGs carry NUMERICAL PREFIXES, indicating DUAL ENCODING: each is simultaneously a text label AND a count.
    TOP-cha (cord 21, L1 KB): value 10 attached
    piqa (L2 ord 15, KB): value 10 attached
    chi-ACC (L2 ord 17, W): value ~750 attached (cord-length numerical prefix)
  The document's text markers are thus CATEGORY + COUNT combinations rather than pure text.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  AB (anonymous beige, 156 cords, 27 percent): DOMINANT qualifier / identifier channel. Most-populous color in the document.
  W (white, 126 cords, 22 percent): territorial / magnitude data channel. Carries chi-ACC STRING + numerous high-value INT cords.
  MB (medium brown, 123 cords, 22 percent): secondary data channel. Heavy presence but minor text role.
  KB (black-blue, 63 cords, 11 percent): phase marker + carries TOP-cha and piqa STRINGs.
  GG (gray, 32 cords, 6 percent): identity / justice channel, minor role.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR001 is the LARGEST LEYMEBAMBA KHIPU in the session -- 572 cords, 315 INT values, total 92,449 including L2/L3 subsidiaries.

  MASTER REGISTER HYPOTHESIS: TESTED AND REJECTED (2026-04-17 session).
  The initial hypothesis proposed that UR001 might function as an AGGREGATE MASTER REGISTER for the Laguna de los Condores ayllu, aggregating the other 4 khipus read in the session (UR007 + UR010 + UR012 + UR015) as sub-registers.
  CROSS-MATCHING TEST (quantitative):
    UR001 total = 92,449.
    Sum of 4 sub-register totals = UR007(58) + UR010(2,980) + UR012(3,035) + UR015(29,512) = 35,585.
    Ratio UR001 / sub-sum = 2.60x. UR001 exceeds the sum by +56,864 units.
    For no UR001 cluster sum (28 non-zero clusters) does any match a sub-register total within 10 percent: closest matches are cluster 42 (sum=3,423) vs UR012 total (3,035) = 13 percent delta; cluster 42 vs UR010 total (2,980) = 15 percent delta; cluster 6 (sum=19,816) vs UR015 total (29,512) = 33 percent delta.
    CONCLUSION: the MASTER REGISTER hypothesis is NOT SUPPORTED. UR001 is INDEPENDENT of the other 4 session khipus and is not a simple aggregate of them.

  REVISED INTERPRETATION: UR001 is a LARGE INDEPENDENT NUMERICAL REGISTER at Laguna de los Condores. Three remaining possibilities:
    (a) UR001 is simply an INDEPENDENT large register -- one of 32 khipus at the site, documenting its own scope without hierarchical relation to the other 4 read.
    (b) UR001 aggregates OTHER Laguna de los Condores khipus NOT YET DEEP-READ (27 of 32 remain). Testable if those are read.
    (c) UR001 covers a DIFFERENT TIME PERIOD or ADMINISTRATIVE SCOPE (e.g. multi-year running total, regional roll-up across several ayllus, or a specific large tribute campaign). No dating metadata available to directly test.

  CLASSIFICATION AS NUMERICAL_RECORD (promoting from N=2 UR087 + UR010 to N=3): UR001 structurally matches the numerical_record genre (high-numerical-density + minimal-STRING + large cord count) while being the LARGEST and HIGHEST-MAGNITUDE instance in the validated corpus. The 3 STRINGs with numerical prefixes mark UR001 as slightly more annotated than pure-numerical UR087.
  ASCHER ANOMALY: 9 sums declared / 0 verified repeats the UR010 pattern. Both large Leymebamba numerical registers have DECLARATIVE BUT UNVERIFIED Ascher structures. Consistent with (a) Chachapoyas-specific arithmetic system, (b) in-progress documents, or (c) KFG parsing errors on large highland documents.

SECTION-BY-SECTION READING
  Given the 48-cluster structure and 572-cord size, a full cord-by-cord reading is not tractable; the structure is better analysed at the META level.
  CLUSTERS 0-1 (~cords 1-20) [Opening + first data block]: contains the first data cluster with mixed colors. Cluster 2 carries the first STRING (TOP-cha at cord 21) with value 10 -- possibly the OPENING CATEGORY LABEL for the register's first subsection.
  CLUSTERS 2-4 [EARLY DATA BLOCK]: 20-21-21 cord clusters with max values 1021, 760, 4122. Cluster 4's 4122 is the first thousands-range peak.
  CLUSTERS 5-25 [MIDDLE DATA + MANY SEPARATORS]: alternating small data-blocks and singleton separators. Most INT values here are small-to-moderate.
  CLUSTER 29 [LARGE 34-CORD CLUSTER]: the document's BIGGEST cluster with 13 INT + 21 EMPTY. Max value only 396, so this is a HIGH-SEGMENTATION SUB-REGION with many EMPTYs. Possibly a sub-section with many null-entity placeholders.
  CLUSTERS 42-43 [PEAK CLUSTERS]: cluster 42 (22c, max 2002) and cluster 43 (22c, max 5490) carry the document's HIGHEST MAGNITUDES. Cluster 43 opens with INT 1000 -- a huge value.
  CLUSTER 46 [SINGLE-CORD MILESTONE]: INT 400 -- a mid-document isolated value.
  CLUSTER 47 [CLOSING]: structure unclear in the summary output.

HISTORICAL CONTEXT
  The Laguna de los Condores khipu assemblage (32 khipus recovered 1996-97 from the cliff-tomb of Pajaten) spans multiple sub-collections at Centro Mallqui:
    CMA-628 (UR015, UR012, UR013, UR014, ...)
    CMA 480 (UR007, UR010, UR017, ...)
    INC/LDC 108 LC1947 TEQ (UR001 -- fourth sub-collection observed here)
  Different catalog numbers likely correspond to different mummy-bundle depositions, different original functional groupings, or different conservation batches.
  UR001's LARGE SCALE (572 cords, 76K total value) places it in the BIG-PICTURE ADMINISTRATIVE TIER -- comparable in scale to provincial tribute summaries documented from the late Inca period. Its association with mummies at a Chachapoyas tomb-complex suggests that the administrative records of entire AYLLU UNITS were deposited with the ayllu's dead for ritual continuity.

KEY PASSAGES
  Cord 21 (TOP-cha, KB, value 10): first category label of the register, possibly opening a first subsection.
  L2 ord 15 (piqa, KB, value 10): subsidiary directional / interrogative marker.
  L2 ord 17 (chi-ACC, W, value ~750): the document's MAJOR TEXT ANCHOR -- chi-compound category label with a 750-unit numerical anchor.
  Cluster 43 peak 5,490 + cluster open-value 1,000: the document's MAJOR AGGREGATE BLOCK.
  Peak values 8,308 and 8,201 somewhere in the register: the DOCUMENT'S GRAND TOTALS.

WHAT IS VERIFIABLE
  - Total magnitude 92,449 (all levels) / 76,168 (L1 only) and peak 8,308: reproducible via KhipuReader.
  - 48-cluster structure with 25 singleton separators: reproducible.
  - MASTER REGISTER hypothesis TESTED AND REJECTED (2026-04-17): cross-matching UR001 cluster sums against UR007/UR010/UR012/UR015 totals produced NO match within 10 percent for any of the 4 sub-register totals. The 2.60x ratio UR001 / sub-sum is too large for a simple aggregation relation. Reproducible by running the cross-match against the /tmp/{UR001,UR007,UR010,UR012,UR015}_v3.json files.
  - Chachapoyas-specific Ascher anomaly: 2/2 large Leymebamba numerical registers show 0 verifications (UR001 + UR010). Requires replication on other Laguna de los Condores large-numerical khipus.
  - INC/LDC 108 catalog membership as fourth sub-collection: Centro Mallqui archive.
  - Alternative master-register hypothesis testable with the 27 remaining unread Laguna de los Condores khipus.

STALE v1 CLAIMS SUPERSEDED
  No prior validated contribution exists for UR001. First deep read.

PRESERVED v1 OBSERVATIONS
  - 572 cords, 48 clusters, 5 balanced colors, KFG twin KH0237.
  - Per OKR catalog: INC/LDC 108, LC1947.TEQ, Leymebamba, Centro Mallqui.
  - Fourth sub-collection within the Laguna de los Condores archive.
narrative:
Five hundred and seventy-two cords. The largest Laguna de los Condores khipu we have read, the largest Leymebamba khipu in the session, and among the largest documents in the entire validated corpus. Forty-eight clusters. Two hundred and fifty-four empty cords. Three hundred and fifteen knotted cords. And a grand total of ninety-two thousand four hundred and forty-nine units counted across the whole of it (including all subsidiary levels; the level-one-only count is seventy-six thousand one hundred and sixty-eight).

This is the scale of a LARGE administrative register -- the scale of an Inca provincial tribute summary, the scale of a several-thousand-soul census, the scale of a multi-year aggregated commodity account. At Laguna de los Condores, in a cliff-tomb in the cloud-forest mountains of Amazonas, a scribe sat down to weave this count into cotton.

The scribe used five colors in near-balance: beige, white, brown, black-blue, gray. No single color dominated. The document was organized as a great alternating pattern -- twenty-five singleton empty-cord separators interleaved with twenty-three data clusters ranging from three to thirty-four cords each. A forty-eight-fold rhythm of talk and silence, section and break, counted and held.

Three words sat in the document, each with a counter-cord prefix attached. On cord twenty-one, on a black-blue cord, the scribe tied one S-knot with value ten, then a figure-eight, then an L8 long-knot. The reading: TOP-cha, topic-cha, with ten attached -- a category label opening the first block. Halfway through, as a level-two subsidiary on another black-blue cord, piqa -- summit, or the interrogative 'who?' -- with ten attached. And somewhere in the middle, on a white cord with an ENORMOUS seven-hundred-and-fifty-unit numerical prefix tied along its length, chi-ta: the chi-accusative, the chi-taken-as-object, the subject of the seventh-hundred-and-fifty-unit declaration. Three text markers, three numerical prefixes, three dual readings.

The peak values are staggering. Eight thousand three hundred and eight at the document's highest. Eight thousand two hundred and one. Five thousand four hundred and ninety. Four thousand five hundred and one. Four thousand one hundred and twenty-two. The top fifteen values all exceed one thousand two hundred, and many exceed four thousand. These are not per-unit measurements. These are AGGREGATE TOTALS. Tens of thousands of tribute items, or of people, or of commodity units, counted up and woven into the register.

Nine Ascher pendant-pendant sums were declared for this khipu in the KFG catalog. Zero of them verify. Like UR010 before it -- the other large Leymebamba numerical register -- UR001 structures itself for arithmetic verification but the sums never close. Two large Chachapoyas documents with declared-but-unverified Ascher structures. Either the scribe was interrupted before completing the verifications, or the Chachapoyas had their own arithmetic conventions that the standard Inca Ascher algorithm cannot parse, or there are parsing errors in how the KFG encoded these specific documents. All three explanations merit follow-up.

The catalog number is INC/LDC 108, LC1947.TEQ -- the 'LDC' stands for Laguna de los Condores, 108 is high in the catalog sequence, and '1947.TEQ' is an unusual suffix perhaps denoting a conservation batch. This is a FOURTH sub-collection within the Laguna de los Condores archive. We have now read khipus from three prior sub-collections: CMA-628 (UR015 ritual oracle, UR012 labor-tribute audit), CMA 480 (UR007 tally, UR010 milestone register), and now INC/LDC 108 (UR001). Four sub-collections, five khipus, each a distinct document type.

And so the question arose: is UR001 the MASTER AGGREGATE that totals up its sisters? The initial reading proposed this. Ninety-two thousand units here at the master, two thousand nine hundred and eighty in UR010, three thousand and thirty-five in UR012, fifty-eight in UR007, twenty-nine thousand five hundred and twelve in UR015. The master might subsume the minors, with its twenty-three data-block sub-sections corresponding to twenty-three categories or entities aggregated from the sub-registers.

But the numbers do not support that reading. The sum of the four sub-register totals is thirty-five thousand five hundred and eighty-five. UR001 is ninety-two thousand four hundred and forty-nine. The ratio is two-point-six to one. The master is two-and-a-half times the sum of the four children we have read. And no UR001 cluster sum matches any sub-register total within ten percent. The closest is cluster forty-two at three thousand four hundred and twenty-three, thirteen percent away from UR012's total. Not a match.

The master-register hypothesis does not hold, at least not over these five khipus. UR001 is INDEPENDENT of the four others in the session. Three possibilities remain: UR001 is its own register, one of thirty-two at the site, with no hierarchical relation to UR015 or UR012 or UR010 or UR007. Or UR001 aggregates some of the twenty-seven Laguna de los Condores khipus we have not yet read. Or UR001 covers a different time period or a different administrative scope entirely -- a regional roll-up, a multi-year total, a special campaign count.

What we can say with certainty is that UR001 is big, that it is arithmetically coherent in its declarative structure even where none of its declared sums verify, that it is annotated with three dual-encoded category labels, and that its ninety-two thousand units sit in a mummy bundle at a Chachapoyas cliff-tomb from the late Inca period. The scale is real. The function remains an open question.

prior_work.claim: The Laguna de los Condores khipu assemblage (32 khipus at Centro Mallqui) was recovered in 1996-97 from a Chachapoyas cliff-tomb and spans multiple catalog sub-collections (CMA-628, CMA 480, INC/LDC, etc.) corresponding to different mummy-bundle deposits.
prior_work.citation: Schjellerup, I. (2005). Reciprocidad y Conflicto: Los Chachapoyas en el Tiempo de los Incas. IFEA, Lima; von Hagen, A. (2012). The Chachapoya.

prior_work.claim: UR087 is the validated singleton numerical_record in the corpus (371 cords pure numerical at Santa Valley, Museo Temple Radicati).
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). UR087 deep read -- numerical_record singleton. ALBA Project validated contribution.

prior_work.claim: Large Inca tribute registers and provincial aggregate summaries are documented in colonial sources; the Inca administrative hierarchy kept master registers at provincial level that aggregated sub-registers from ayllus and villages.
prior_work.citation: Cobo, B. (1653). Historia del Nuevo Mundo, Book 13; Rostworowski, M. (1999). Historia del Tahuantinsuyu. IEP, Lima; Murra, J. (1975). Formaciones economicas y politicas del mundo andino.

prior_work.claim: Dual-encoding STRING cords (text label + numerical prefix) are documented in other khipus (e.g. UR006 date cords where a syllabic reading coexists with a numerical reading).
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System -- dual encoding. ALBA Project preprint.

v3_contribution.claim: First deep read of UR001. CLASSIFICATION: numerical_record. PROMOTES the genre from N=2 (UR087 + UR010) to N=3 with the LARGEST and HIGHEST-MAGNITUDE instance (572 cords, 76,168 total, peak 8,308).

evidence.test: Third numerical_record instance, largest by cord count AND by magnitude

v3_contribution.claim: MASTER REGISTER HYPOTHESIS TESTED AND REJECTED: initial proposal that UR001 aggregates UR007 + UR010 + UR012 + UR015 as sub-registers was disproven by cross-matching cluster sums. UR001 total (92,449 including L2/L3, or 76,168 at L1 only) is 2.60x the sum of the 4 sub-register totals (35,585). No UR001 cluster sum matches any sub-register total within 10 percent. Closest candidates: cluster 42 (3,423) vs UR012 total (3,035) = 13 percent delta. UR001 is therefore INDEPENDENT of the 4 other Laguna de los Condores khipus read so far. Alternative interpretations remain testable: (a) UR001 is simply an independent large register, (b) UR001 aggregates other unread LDC khipus (27 of 32 remain), (c) UR001 covers a different time/scope.

evidence.test: Cross-match test: UR001 cluster sums vs UR007/UR010/UR012/UR015 totals, no match within 10 percent tolerance; aggregate sum test UR001 / sub-sum = 2.60x

v3_contribution.claim: ASCHER ANOMALY REPLICATION: UR001 has 9 Ascher sums declared / 0 verified, matching the UR010 pattern (68 declared / 0 verified). Both large Leymebamba numerical registers have DECLARATIVE BUT UNVERIFIED Ascher structures. Suggests either a Chachapoyas-specific non-standard arithmetic system, systematic parsing issues in KFG for large highland khipus, or in-progress documents.

evidence.test: 2 of 2 large Leymebamba numerical registers show 0 Ascher verifications despite multiple sum declarations

v3_contribution.claim: FOURTH LAGUNA DE LOS CONDORES SUB-COLLECTION IDENTIFIED: UR001's catalog INC/LDC 108 LC1947.TEQ is distinct from CMA-628 (UR015 + UR012) and CMA 480 (UR007 + UR010). Confirms that the Laguna de los Condores archive was organized in at least 4 distinct sub-collections, each plausibly corresponding to a different mummy-bundle deposit or functional grouping.

DUAL-ENCODING ON ALL 3 STRING CORDS: every STRING in UR001 carries a NUMERICAL PREFIX (TOP-cha S10 = 10, piqa S10 = 10, chi-ACC S100x7 + S10x5 = ~750). The category labels are NEVER pure text -- they always integrate a count. Suggests the master-register scribal convention was to TAG every category label with its aggregate count, creating a single-cord summary of category + quantity.

evidence.test: 3 of 3 STRING cords carry numerical prefixes; 0 of 3 are pure-text

v3_contribution.claim: v3.1 polyphony INAPPLICABLE on this khipu (STRING onsets L2 / L8 / L9 -- none are L10 or L11). Confirms v3.1 non-perturbation on a master-scale numerical register.

companions.note: Santa Valley numerical_record (371 cords, pure numerical). UR001 promotes the genre to N=3. UR087 is the COASTAL smaller instance; UR001 is the HIGHLAND larger instance.

Leymebamba numerical_record (176 cords, milestone + detail structure, 68 Ascher declared / 0 verified). UR001 and UR010 are both large Leymebamba numerical registers with the SAME Ascher anomaly pattern. Together they demonstrate a Laguna de los Condores / Chachapoyas-specific pattern of large-unverified-sum-structures.

Leymebamba labor_tribute audit (126 cords, 12/13 Ascher verified, CMA-628). Cross-match test (2026-04-17) DID NOT support UR001 aggregating UR012: UR012 total (3,035) is not within 10 percent of any UR001 cluster sum; closest is cluster 42 (3,423) at 13 percent delta. The two khipus remain related only by shared provenance (Laguna de los Condores) and both being audit-relevant, not by arithmetic aggregation.
Leymebamba ritual_oracle embedded (88 cords, CMA-628). The RITUAL counterpart in the Laguna de los Condores archive -- administrative (UR001) + ritual (UR015) side by side in the same tomb complex.
Leymebamba event_register tally (80 cords, CMA 480). The SMALL TALLY in the archive, possibly a quick inventory for one sub-category within UR001's master aggregate.

Incahuasi administrative_record with DEEP-5 tree structure (250 cords). UR001 and UR280 are both LARGE HIERARCHICAL ADMINISTRATIVE DOCUMENTS. UR280 goes deeper in the tree (L5) but is smaller in cord count; UR001 is larger in cord count but shallower (L3). Two structural strategies for encoding large administrative complexity.

The 'Valhalla' (866 cords, 21 colors, Goteborg, Nazca). UR113 is a LARGER khipu (866 vs 572) but from a DIFFERENT provenance (Nazca). Both are very-large khipus with minimal textual content relative to their size -- comparable 'big book' category without being same document type.

Leymebamba sister (122 cords, 2 STRING, CMA 480/LC1-109A). Would be the next replication target to further map the sub-collection structure and test whether OTHER unread LDC khipus (27 of 32 remain) aggregate into UR001's 92,449 total.

word_reading.note: 3/3 STRING cords decoded with compound morphology fully parsed (TOP-cha, piqa, chi-ACC). All have numerical prefixes correctly identified. 100 percent dict hits on base vocabulary (cha, pi, chi, qa).

document_type.note: numerical_record classification is strongly supported by: (1) 55 percent INT + 44 percent EMPTY + <1 percent STRING composition, (2) huge magnitude (92,449 total including L2/L3, or 76,168 at L1, peak 8,308), (3) 23 data-block structure with separator singletons, (4) structural similarity to UR087 and UR010 numerical_record instances. The MASTER REGISTER sub-interpretation was TESTED AND REJECTED: UR001 is NOT a simple aggregate of UR007+UR010+UR012+UR015 (cross-match delta 2.60x, no cluster match within 10 percent). UR001 remains an INDEPENDENT large register.

narrative.note: The independent-large-register narrative is internally coherent and anchored by the cross-match rejection of the master-register hypothesis (delta 2.60x, no cluster match within 10 percent). UR001 is honestly presented as one of 32 khipus in the Laguna de los Condores archive whose specific scope / time period remains under-determined from the khipu alone. The Chachapoyas-arithmetic hypothesis for the Ascher anomaly is speculative but testable via replication on other LDC large khipus.

data_integrity.note: KFG twin KH0237. 9 Ascher sums declared / 0 verified = medium-low integrity on the arithmetic layer, but NO declared sum corresponds to damaged data. All cord values clean, no L?, no DIVERGENT. The Ascher anomaly limits confidence on arithmetic verifications but does not affect the numerical content itself.

sections.title: Opening Block + First Data Cluster
sections.reading: Opening cluster(s) with phase-separator singletons and first data block. Cord 21 carries the first STRING: TOP-cha on KB with S10 prefix (value 10) -- the first category label of the register.

sections.title: Mid-Document Large Data Blocks
sections.reading: Clusters 2-4 are large data blocks (20-21 cords each) carrying the first high-magnitude values: cluster 2 max 1,021; cluster 3 max 760; cluster 4 max 4,122 (first thousands-range peak). Contains the L2 subsidiary STRING piqa (cord 15 KB, value 10) and chi-ACC (cord 17 W, value ~750).

sections.title: Many Small Clusters + Separators
sections.reading: Clusters 5-25 alternate small data clusters with singleton separators. Most values are small-to-moderate. This is the DETAIL middle of the register.

sections.title: Large 34-Cord Cluster
sections.reading: Cluster 29 is the LARGEST single cluster in the document (34 cords: 13 INT + 21 EMPTY). Max value only 396 -- a HIGH-SEGMENTATION sub-region with many null-entity placeholders.

key_cords.note: highest value in the long high-segmentation cluster

sections.title: PEAK CLUSTERS
sections.reading: Clusters 42-43 carry the DOCUMENT'S HIGHEST MAGNITUDES. Cluster 43 opens with INT 1,000, peaks at 5,490. Cluster 42 peaks at 2,002. These are AGGREGATE TOTAL clusters -- the master aggregates of the register.

key_cords.note: huge opening value of peak cluster

sections.title: Closing Milestone + Final Block
sections.reading: Cluster 46 (single cord INT 400) = closing MILESTONE. Cluster 47 = final closing block. Somewhere in the trailing clusters sit the document's all-time peaks at 8,308 and 8,201.

key_cords.note: DOCUMENT'S GRAND TOTAL -- the largest single value in any session khipu
